basic_info.title: Final Fantasy VI
basic_info.review: Final Fantasy VI is a masterpiece of storytelling and character development, featuring a diverse cast and a narrative that transcends the typical fantasy tropes of its time.

play_today.details: Widely considered the definitive version by the community. Features improved translation, additional content including four new Espers (Leviathan, Gilgamesh, Cactuar, Diabolos), new areas like Dragons' Den and Soul Shrine, three new spells, bug fixes, bestiary, music player, and character portraits during dialogue.

recommended_patches.name: Final Fantasy VI Retranslated
recommended_patches.description: Large-scale script rewrite based on Clyde Mandelin's live translation stream from Legends of Localization. Brings the text closer to original Japanese while maintaining readability.

recommended_patches.name: Ted Woolsey Uncensored Edition
recommended_patches.description: Restores censored content from the original SNES translation including nudity, religious references, and violent expressions that were removed for North American release.

recommended_patches.name: T-Edition + EX (English Translation)
recommended_patches.description: Popular Japanese ROM hack ported to English. Features rebalanced gameplay, new spells, attacks, items, difficulty modifications, and bug fixes for an alternate universe experience.

recommended_patches.name: Color Restoration Patch
recommended_patches.description: Restores original SNES color palette to GBA version while keeping improved translation and additional content.

optimal_settings: Enable sound interpolation for better audio quality

play_today.details: Modern official remaster with new 16-bit sprites, remastered soundtrack featuring real opera singers for the Aria di Mezzo Carattere, quality of life improvements including auto-battle and eight-directional movement. Best legal option without emulation.

recommended_patches.name: Pixel Remaster Mods (NexusMods)
recommended_patches.description: Various community mods including vibrant ReShade presets, font replacements to match console versions, and quality of life improvements.

play_today.details: Original authentic experience with classic Ted Woolsey translation. Best for purists who want the original SNES presentation without GBA additions.

recommended_patches.name: Final Fantasy VI Retranslated
recommended_patches.description: Improved translation patch for SNES version based on Legends of Localization live stream.

recommended_patches.name: Ted Woolsey Uncensored Edition
recommended_patches.description: Restores censored content from original SNES translation.

optimal_settings: Enable Super FX chip emulation if needed

play_today.details: Features FMV opening and ending sequences, memo save feature for quick saves, bestiary, artwork gallery. Translation remains same as SNES version.

The definitive modern experience via the 2024 Pixel Remaster. Features native 4K/1080p support, widescreen aspect ratios, and seamless cloud saves. Includes the GBA version's extra content like the new dungeon and voice acting. Offers modern controller support, quick-save functionality, and a fully localized English experience without the need for emulation or patches.

recommended_patches.name: Final Fantasy VI Pixel Remaster Widescreen Fix
recommended_patches.description: A community patch for the Windows Pixel Remaster that forces true widescreen aspect ratios and fixes UI scaling issues on ultrawide monitors.

recommended_patches.name: FF6 Pixel Remaster Quality of Life Mod
recommended_patches.description: Adds quick-save functionality, auto-map updates, and inventory sorting to the Windows Pixel Remaster version.

play_today.details: The purist's choice for the original 1994 experience. Features the iconic, unrearranged soundtrack by Nobuo Uematsu and the original sprite art. While the North American release has censorship, the 'Ted Woolsey Uncensored' fan patch restores the original script. Requires emulation for modern convenience, but offers the most faithful representation of the game's initial design and difficulty.

recommended_patches.name: Final Fantasy VI: Ted Woolsey Uncensored Edition
recommended_patches.description: Restores the original uncensored script and dialogue removed from the North American SNES release, including the Lizard Man sprite and original text.

recommended_patches.name: Final Fantasy VI: Revised Old Style Edition
recommended_patches.description: A comprehensive retranslation for the SNES version that combines the accuracy of the GBA script with the pacing and energy of the original SNES localization.

recommended_patches.name: Final Fantasy VI: Hope Eternal and Love Everlasting
recommended_patches.description: A complete overhaul of the SNES version that polishes gameplay, story, and graphics while retaining the original spirit, featuring updated character sprites and UI.

recommended_patches.name: Final Fantasy VI: T-Edition
recommended_patches.description: A difficulty and content expansion patch for the SNES version that adds new dungeons, enemies, and a harder difficulty mode for experienced players.

optimal_settings: Set Internal Resolution to 4x or 8x for sharp scaling
Enable 'NTSC' mode for accurate color timing
Set 'Frame Skip' to 0 for smooth animation
Use 'Original Sound' to preserve the authentic soundtrack
Map 'Touch Controls' to match original button layout
Set 'Frame Skip' to 1 if experiencing lag
Enable 'CRT Filter' for retro visual style
Set 'Internal Resolution' to 2x or 4x for clean retro look
Configure 'Save States' to auto-save every 5 minutes
Use 'Original Sound' for accurate audio reproduction

play_today.details: A solid middle ground offering the original SNES gameplay with added content like the new dungeon and voice acting. However, the soundtrack is heavily rearranged and often criticized for being inferior to the original. The screen resolution is limited, and the controls can feel cramped. Best for players who want the extra content without the modern remaster's changes.

recommended_patches.name: FF6 GBA Widescreen Patch
recommended_patches.description: A patch for the Game Boy Advance version that enables widescreen rendering when played on emulators, stretching the game to fit modern aspect ratios.

recommended_patches.name: FF6 GBA Bugfix Patch
recommended_patches.description: Fixes known bugs in the Game Boy Advance version, including the 'Lucca's Gun' glitch and other minor gameplay issues.

optimal_settings: Enable 'Widescreen Patch' for modern aspect ratios
Set 'Frame Rate' to 60Hz for smooth gameplay
Use 'Original Sound' to preserve the GBA soundtrack
Map 'Touch Controls' to match original button layout
Set 'Frame Skip' to 0 for smooth animation
Enable 'CRT Filter' for retro visual style

description.synopsis: In a world where magic and technology coexist, a group of rebels fights against the tyrannical Empire of Gestahl, which seeks to harness the power of magic through Magitek technology. After a catastrophic event destroys the world, the survivors must navigate a ruined landscape to defeat the mad mage Kefka, who has ascended to godhood and threatens to plunge the world into eternal darkness.
key_features: Active Time Battle (ATB) system with real-time movement
Large ensemble cast of 14 playable characters with unique abilities
Non-linear world map exploration with multiple entry points
Complex narrative structure with a mid-game world destruction event
Job System allowing character class customization and skill mastery
Magitek Armor mechs as both enemies and playable units
Dynamic orchestral soundtrack by Nobuo Uematsu
Multiple endings based on player choices and character survival
Strategic use of magic and items in turn-based combat
Deep character development arcs for each party member
legacy_and_impact: Final Fantasy VI is widely regarded as one of the greatest video games of all time, credited with elevating the narrative depth of the JRPG genre. Its ensemble cast and complex themes of industrialization and mental health set a new benchmark for storytelling in gaming. The game's influence is evident in the design of subsequent RPGs, particularly in the use of large party sizes and non-linear progression.
